functional_requirements.requirement: Calculate budget balance by summing advert costs, adding overheads percentage, and subtracting from planned budget.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: This is calculated by summing the cost of all the adverts in a campaign, adding a percentage for overheads and subtracting the result from the planned budget.

functional_requirements.requirement: Display client list in Client dropdown when user selects a client.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: The Client dropdown displays a list of clients. When a client is selected, their campaigns will be displayed in the Campaign dropdown.

functional_requirements.requirement: Enable Campaign dropdown with campaign list after client selection.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: When a client is selected, a list of campaigns is loaded into the Campaign dropdown.

functional_requirements.requirement: Enable Check button when user selects a campaign.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: The Campaign dropdown displays a list of campaigns belonging to the client selected in the Client dropdown. When a campaign is selected the Check button is enabled.

functional_requirements.requirement: Display budget calculation result in Budget textfield.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: The Budget textfield displays the result of the calculation to check the budget.

functional_requirements.requirement: Perform budget balance calculation when user clicks Check button.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: The Check button causes the calculation of the budget balance to take place.

functional_requirements.requirement: Clear Budget textfield when user selects a new client or campaign.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: It is cleared whenever a new client is selected or a new campaign is selected. It is not editable.

functional_requirements.requirement: Disable Check button when user selects a new client.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: The Check button is disabled whenever a new client is selected.

functional_requirements.requirement: Display alert dialogue with 'Close window? Are you sure?' message and OK/Cancel buttons when user clicks Close button.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: When the Close button is clicked, an alert dialogue is displayed. This asks ‘Close window? Are you sure?’ and displays two buttons labelled OK and Cancel.

functional_requirements.requirement: Exit use case when user clicks OK in alert dialogue.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If OK is clicked the window is exited; if Cancel is clicked then it carries on in the state it was in before the close button was clicked.

prompt:
You are a requirements engineer.
Extract functional software requirements from the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "functional_requirements": [
    {
      "requirement": "string",
      "evidence_quote": "short exact quote from source text"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
1. Include only system capabilities/behaviors.
2. Exclude background context, impact statements, problem motivation, and pure business goals.
3. Keep each requirement atomic and clear.
4. Preserve original meaning. Do not invent functionality.
5. If uncertain, keep the item but keep evidence_quote explicit.

Source text:
<<<
The requirement here is that the users must be able to check whether the budget for an
advertising campaign has been exceeded or not. This is calculated by summing the cost of all
the adverts in a campaign, adding a percentage for overheads and subtracting the result from
the planned budget. A negative value indicates that the budget has been overspent. This
information is used by a campaign manager.
2. Describe the user interface behaviour
There are five active elements of the user interface: the Client dropdown, the Campaign
dropdown, the Budget textfield, the Check button and the Close button. These are shown in
Fig. 17.6.
The Client dropdown displays a list of clients. When a client is selected, their campaigns
will be displayed in the Campaign dropdown.
The Campaign dropdown displays a list of campaigns belonging to the client selected in
the Client dropdown. When a campaign is selected the Check button is enabled.
The Budget textfield displays the result of the calculation to check the budget.
The Check button causes the calculation of the budget balance to take place.
The Close button closes the window and exits the use case.
3. Define user interface rules
User interface objects with constant behaviour
The Client dropdown has constant behaviour. Whenever a client is selected, a list of
campaigns is loaded into the Campaign dropdown.
The Budget textfield is initially empty. It is cleared whenever a new client is selected
or a new campaign is selected. It is not editable.
The Close button may be pressed at any time to close the window.
User interface objects with varying behaviour
The Campaign dropdown is initially disabled. No campaign can be selected until a
client has been selected. Once it has been loaded with a list of campaigns it is
enabled.
The Check button is initially disabled. It is enabled when a campaign is selected. It is
disabled whenever a new client is selected.
Entry and exit events
The window is entered from the main window when the Check Campaign Budget
menu item is selected.
When the Close button is clicked, an alert dialogue is displayed. This asks ‘Close
window? Are you sure?’ and displays two buttons labelled OK and Cancel. If OK is
clicked the window is exited; if Cancel is clicked then it carries on in the state it was
in before the close button was clicked.
>>>
